No Is the thing you're thinking of a concept or an idea?

top_comment.author: aphyer
top_comment.text: This part is actually fascinating.  If this were a human I would imagine that they were trolling you.  Here I'm wondering whether it gets through strawberry-cherry and then decides that it must have already guessed all the common possibilities earlier, and starts going through weirder possibilities.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: Here I'm wondering whether it gets through strawberry-cherry and then decides that it must have already guessed all the common possibilities earlier, and starts going through weirder possibilities.

summary: The main claim is: No single metaphor can capture all of AI’s essential elements, and we should be careful of over-interpreting metaphors.
I also believe that we do not understand AI enough to make confident predictions or analogs , so we should take any metaphor with a grain of salt.

The main claim is: The main particular thing I mean to actively exclude by stipulating that the system must be agentic is an LLM or similar system arguing for itself to be improved in response to a prompt.
The main claim is: The amount of compute required to emulate the human brain depends on the level of detail we want to emulate.
The main claim is: Deception What kind of interpretability tools do we need to avoid deception?

The main claim is: This curriculum, a follow-up to the Alignment Fundamentals curriculum (the ‘101)’ to this 201 curriculum), aims to give participants enough knowledge about alignment to understand the frontier of current research discussions.

It assumes that participants have read through the Alignment Fundamentals curriculum, taken a course on deep learning, and taken a course on reinforcement learning (or have an equivalent level of knowledge).

The main claim is: I think it's definitely worth knowing about this stuff, since there are a lot of good ideas, and a lot of the hard work of alignment is about just trying to get any technical angle of attack on the problem.
The main claim is: There exists a much larger secondary market for used commodity hardware than for AI-specialized hardware.
Existing supercomputers) run on "commodity hardware that we might leverage for AI applications.
The main claim is: As we can see, the number of FLOP for linear layers is 2x their number of parameters.
Convolutional layers have many more FLOP for passes than for weight updates.
The main claim is: Another notion of “relevance” is causal influence - while probabilistic information is the key criteria for prediction, causal influence is the key for planning.

If I’m planning a roadtrip from San Francisco to Los Angeles, the details of my route through the Bay Area are irrelevant to planning my route within LA - except for the high-level information contained in my choice of highway for the midd…
